subject: AWS
description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a comprehensive cloud computing platform offering infrastructure-as-a-service, platform-as-a-service, and software-as-a-service solutions.

questions.question: What is the difference between an AWS Region and an Availability Zone (AZ)?
questions.answer:
AWS Region:
- Separate geographical area (e.g., N. Virginia, Ireland)
- Contains multiple Availability Zones
- Independent and isolated

Availability Zone (AZ):
- One or more data centers within a Region
- Independent power and networking
- Connected via low-latency links

Deploy across multiple AZs for high availability.

questions.question: Explain the AWS Shared Responsibility Model.
questions.answer:
AWS manages:
- Physical hardware and data centers
- Network infrastructure
- Virtualization layer

Customer manages:
- Data encryption and access
- IAM policies and access
- Application security
- OS patches (for EC2)

By service type:
- IaaS: Customer manages OS and data
- PaaS: AWS manages platform
- SaaS: AWS manages everything

questions.question: What is an EC2 instance and instance type?
questions.answer:
EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud) provides resizable compute capacity.

Instance Types:
- General Purpose (T, M): Balanced CPU/memory
- Compute Optimized (C): High CPU
- Memory Optimized (R, X): Large RAM
- Storage Optimized (I, D): Fast disk
- GPU (P, G): ML/graphics

Pricing:
- On-Demand: Pay per second
- Reserved: 1-3 year commitment
- Spot: Interruptible, cheap

questions.question: What is Amazon S3 and what guarantees does it provide?
questions.answer:
S3 (Simple Storage Service) is object storage.

Guarantees:
- 11 nines (99.999999999%) durability
- 99.99% availability (Standard)

Storage Classes:
- S3 Standard: Frequent access
- S3 IA: Infrequent (lower cost)
- S3 Glacier: Archival
- S3 Intelligent-Tiering: Auto-optimizes

questions.question: What are the core VPC components?
questions.answer:
VPC Core Components:

1. Subnets: Public & private networks
2. Route Tables: Traffic routing rules
3. Internet Gateway (IGW): Internet access
4. NAT Gateway: Private subnet internet access
5. Security Groups: Stateful instance firewall
6. NACLs: Stateless subnet firewall
7. VPC Peering: Direct VPC-to-VPC connection

questions.question: What is IAM and why is it critical?
questions.answer:
IAM (Identity and Access Management) controls AWS access.

Key Concepts:
- Users: Individual people
- Groups: Collection of users
- Roles: Temporary permissions
- Policies: JSON permissions documents

Best Practices:
- Principle of least privilege
- Enable MFA
- Rotate access keys regularly

questions.question: What is the difference between stopping and terminating EC2?
questions.answer:
Stop:
- Shuts down gracefully
- Preserves root EBS volume
- Can restart later
- No compute charges

Terminate:
- Permanently deletes instance
- Root EBS deleted by default
- Cannot restart
- All charges stop

questions.question: What is Amazon RDS?
questions.answer:
RDS (Relational Database Service) is managed database.

Supported Engines:
- PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB
- Oracle, SQL Server
- Aurora (AWS-native)

Benefits:
- Automated backups and patching
- Multi-AZ deployment
- Read replicas for scaling

questions.question: What is the difference between Lambda and EC2?
questions.answer:
Lambda (Serverless):
- No server management
- Event-driven execution
- Pay per 100ms invocation
- Max 15 min execution
- Cold starts possible

EC2 (Virtual Servers):
- Full control over server
- Long-running applications
- Pay per second/hour
- You manage scaling

questions.question: What is Amazon DynamoDB?
questions.answer:
DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database.

Features:
- Key-value and document data
- Serverless or provisioned capacity
- Single-digit millisecond latency
- Automatic global tables
- Encryption at rest

questions.question: What is the difference between Security Groups and NACLs?
questions.answer:
Security Groups (Stateful):
- Instance-level firewall
- Default: deny all inbound
- Only allow rules

NACLs (Stateless):
- Subnet-level firewall
- Default: allow all
- Allow AND deny rules
- Rules evaluated by number

questions.question: What is AWS CloudFront?
questions.answer:
CloudFront is AWS CDN.

Features:
- Global edge locations
- Low latency delivery
- DDoS protection (Shield)
- SSL/TLS encryption
- Cache content at edge

Origins:
- S3 bucket
- EC2 instance
- ALB

questions.question: What is Amazon EKS?
questions.answer:
EKS (Elastic Kubernetes Service) is managed Kubernetes.

Benefits:
- Managed control plane
- Integration with AWS services
- VPC-native networking
- Load balancing with ALB

Use when:
- Running containerized apps
- Using Kubernetes
- Need multi-cloud portability

questions.question: What is Amazon ECS?
questions.answer:
ECS (Elastic Container Service) is AWS container orchestration.

Launch Types:
- Fargate: Serverless
- EC2: You manage instances

Features:
- Native AWS integration
- ELB integration
- IAM task roles

questions.question: What is AWS Auto Scaling?
questions.answer:
Auto Scaling automatically adjusts capacity.

Components:
- Auto Scaling Group (ASG)
- Launch Template
- Scaling Policies

Types:
- Target tracking: CPU at 50%
- Step scaling: Based on metrics
- Scheduled: Time-based

questions.question: What is Amazon SNS?
questions.answer:
SNS (Simple Notification Service) is pub/sub messaging.

Features:
- Publish to topics
- Multiple subscribers:
  - SQS queues
  - Email
  - HTTP endpoints
  - Lambda
- Mobile push, SMS

questions.question: What is Amazon SQS?
questions.answer:
SQS (Simple Queue Service) is managed message queuing.

Types:
- Standard: At-least-once delivery
- FIFO: Exactly-once, ordered

Features:
- Decouple microservices
- Buffer database writes
- Dead letter queues

questions.question: What is AWS CloudWatch?
questions.answer:
CloudWatch is monitoring and observability.

Features:
- Metrics: CPU, memory, custom
- Logs: Centralized storage
- Alarms: Threshold-based alerts
- Events: Automated responses
- Dashboards: Visualizations

questions.question: What is AWS IAM Roles and when to use them?
questions.answer:
IAM Roles provide temporary credentials.

Use cases:
- EC2 accessing S3
- Lambda accessing DynamoDB
- Cross-account access
- Federation users

How it works:
1. Attach role with policy
2. Entity assumes role
3. Gets temp credentials
4. Access expires automatically

questions.question: What is Amazon Route 53?
questions.answer:
Route 53 is AWS DNS and domain management.

Services:
- Domain registration
- DNS routing
- Health checking

Routing Policies:
- Simple: Single resource
- Weighted: Traffic split
- Latency-based: Lowest latency
- Geolocation: User location
- Failover: Primary/secondary

questions.question: What is AWS Elastic Beanstalk?
questions.answer:
Elastic Beanstalk is Platform as a Service.

Features:
- Deploy without managing infra
- Supports: Node.js, Python, Java, .NET, Go, Docker
- Managed capacity, load balancing, scaling

Workflow:
1. Upload application code
2. Beanstalk provisions resources
3. Deployed automatically

questions.question: What is Amazon Aurora?
questions.answer:
Aurora is MySQL/PostgreSQL-compatible database.

Features:
- 5x faster than standard MySQL
- Auto-scaling storage (up to 64TB)
- Up to 15 read replicas
- Continuous backup to S3
- Multi-AZ with automatic failover

questions.question: What is AWS Lambda and its limitations?
questions.answer:
Lambda is serverless compute.

Limits:
- Execution time: 15 minutes max
- Memory: 128MB to 10GB
- Payload: 6MB (sync)
- Concurrent: 1000 (adjustable)
- Deployment size: 50MB zipped

Supported:
Node.js, Python, Java, Go, C#, Ruby

questions.question: What is Amazon API Gateway?
questions.answer:
API Gateway creates REST, WebSocket, HTTP APIs.

Features:
- Serverless backend for Lambda
- Throttling and rate limiting
- JWT authentication
- API versioning

Types:
- REST API: Full features
- HTTP API: Lightweight
- WebSocket: Real-time

questions.question: What is AWS CloudFormation?
questions.answer:
CloudFormation is Infrastructure as Code.

Features:
- Declarative JSON/YAML templates
- Provision AWS resources
- Stack lifecycle management
- Drift detection
- Nested stacks

Benefits:
- Reproducible environments
- Version control
- Automated provisioning

questions.question: What is AWS CodePipeline?
questions.answer:
CodePipeline is continuous delivery.

Stages:
- Source: CodeCommit, S3, GitHub
- Build: CodeBuild, Jenkins
- Test: CodeBuild
- Deploy: CodeDeploy, ECS, Beanstalk

Features:
- Visual workflow editor
- Approval gates

questions.question: What is AWS Direct Connect?
questions.answer:
Direct Connect creates private connection to AWS.

Benefits:
- Dedicated connection
- Lower latency
- Consistent performance
- Reduced bandwidth costs

Options:
- Dedicated Connection
- Hosted Connection (via partner)

questions.question: What is Amazon VPC Endpoint?
questions.answer:
VPC Endpoints allow private access to AWS services.

Types:
1. Interface Endpoints:
   - ENI with private IP
   - Many AWS services

2. Gateway Endpoints:
   - S3 and DynamoDB only
   - Route table entry
   - No cost

questions.question: What is AWS WAF and Shield?
questions.answer:
WAF (Web Application Firewall) protects web apps.

Features:
- SQL injection, XSS protection
- Geo-blocking
- Rate limiting
- AWS Managed Rules

Shield:
- DDoS protection
- Standard: Free, always on
- Advanced: Paid

questions.question: What is Amazon Cognito?
questions.answer:
Cognito provides user authentication.

Features:
- Sign-up and sign-in
- Social identity providers
- SAML/Federation
- JWT token issuance

Types:
- User Pools: App authentication
- Identity Pools: AWS resource access

questions.question: What is Amazon ElastiCache?
questions.answer:
ElastiCache is in-memory caching.

Engines:
- Redis: Complex data structures, persistence
- Memcached: Simple, multi-threaded

Use cases:
- Session storage
- Database query cache
- Real-time leaderboards

questions.question: What is AWS Secrets Manager?
questions.answer:
Secrets Manager securely stores secrets.

Features:
- Database credentials
- API keys, OAuth tokens
- Automatic rotation
- Encryption at rest
- Fine-grained IAM

Integration:
- RDS, Redshift, Lambda

questions.question: What is Amazon Redshift?
questions.answer:
Redshift is data warehouse.

Features:
- Columnar storage
- Massively parallel (MPP)
- SQL interface
- ML integration
- Serverless option

Use for:
- Business intelligence
- Analytics

questions.question: What is AWS Global Accelerator?
questions.answer:
Global Accelerator improves app availability.

Features:
- Anycast IP addresses
- Traffic via AWS edge
- Automatic failover
- Fixed IP addresses
- Health checks integration

Use for:
- Global applications
- UDP/TCP traffic

questions.question: What is Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration?
questions.answer:
S3 Transfer Acceleration uses CloudFront.

How it works:
1. Upload to edge location
2. Transfer over AWS network
3. Reach S3 bucket

Benefits:
- Faster uploads globally
- No code changes

questions.question: What is AWS Step Functions?
questions.answer:
Step Functions is serverless workflow.

Features:
- Visual workflow designer
- JSON-based state machine
- Built-in error handling
- Parallel execution

Integrates with:
- Lambda
- ECS
- DynamoDB

questions.question: What is Amazon EventBridge?
questions.answer:
EventBridge is serverless event bus.

Features:
- Connect apps with events
- Schema registry
- Built-in AWS events
- Custom events
- Scheduled rules

Use for:
- Decoupled microservices
- Automated workflows

questions.question: What is AWS Systems Manager?
questions.answer:
Systems Manager manages AWS resources.

Capabilities:
- Run Command: Execute scripts
- Session Manager: Browser SSH/RDP
- Parameter Store: Config storage
- Automation: Workflows
- Patch Manager: OS patching

questions.question: What is Amazon Kinesis?
questions.answer:
Kinesis is real-time data streaming.

Services:
- Data Streams: Build custom apps
- Data Firehose: Load to storage
- Data Analytics: SQL queries

Use for:
- Log processing
- Analytics
- IoT telemetry

questions.question: What is AWS CodeBuild?
questions.answer:
CodeBuild is fully managed build.

Features:
- Code compilation
- Unit test execution
- Artifact generation
- Pay per minute
- Pre-built environments

buildspec.yml:
version: 0.2
phases:
  build:
    commands:
      - npm install

questions.question: What is Amazon EFS?
questions.answer:
EFS is scalable file storage for EC2.

Features:
- NFSv4 protocol
- Elastic capacity
- Multi-AZ storage
- Shared between instances
- Pay per use

Use for:
- Web servers
- CMS
- Shared data

questions.question: What is Amazon FSx?
questions.answer:
FSx provides managed file systems.

Options:
- FSx for Windows File Server
- FSx for Lustre (HPC)
- FSx for NetApp ONTAP
- FSx for OpenZFS

Use for:
- Windows workloads
- High-performance computing

questions.question: What is AWS Organizations?
questions.answer:
Organizations manages multiple AWS accounts.

Features:
- Hierarchical structure (OUs)
- Consolidated billing
- Service Control Policies (SCPs)

Benefits:
- Simplified billing
- Policy-based governance

questions.question: What is AWS Config?
questions.answer:
AWS Config provides resource tracking.

Features:
- Resource inventory
- Configuration history
- Change notifications
- Compliance checking
- Rules (managed/custom)

Use for:
- Security auditing
- Compliance tracking

questions.question: What is AWS Control Tower?
questions.answer:
Control Tower sets up secure multi-account AWS.

Features:
- Landing zone creation
- Guardrails
- Account factory
- Dashboard

Landing Zone:
- OU structure
- Shared services
- Logging account

questions.question: What is AWS Storage Gateway?
questions.answer:
Storage Gateway connects on-prem to AWS storage.

Types:
- File Gateway: NFS/SMB to S3
- Volume Gateway: iSCSI to EBS
- Tape Gateway: VTL to S3/Glacier

Use for:
- Cloud backup
- Hybrid storage
- Migration

questions.question: What is AWS DataSync?
questions.answer:
DataSync is online data transfer.

Features:
- S3, EFS, FSx support
- Scheduled or on-demand
- Bandwidth control
- Encryption in transit
- Verification

Use for:
- Migration
- Replication

questions.question: What is AWS Lake Formation?
questions.answer:
Lake Formation builds secure data lakes.

Features:
- Automated data ingestion
- Fine-grained access control
- Column-level security
- Built on S3 and Glue

Use for:
- Data analytics
- ML data preparation

questions.question: What is AWS Well-Architected Framework?
questions.answer:
Well-Architected Framework guides cloud architecture.

Pillars:
1. Operational Excellence
2. Security
3. Reliability
4. Performance Efficiency
5. Cost Optimization
6. Sustainability

Use to:
- Evaluate architectures
- Improve designs

questions.question: What is Amazon Cognito User Pools vs Identity Pools?
questions.answer:
User Pools:
- User directory
- Sign-up, sign-in
- App authentication
- JWT tokens

Identity Pools:
- AWS credentials
- Access to AWS resources
- Unauthenticated/guest access

Can use both together.
